text: In this article, some institutional aspects of the Kyiv Theological Academy have been presented, which was an educational institution of high importance for spiritual education, scientific research, culture and education of Ukraine and the Russian Empire. It was shown that the Kyiv Theological Academy worked as “personnel smithy” in order to provide spiritual and secular educational institutions, state bodies and local self-governments. The reforms in the education system of the Russian Empire led to creating of the spiritualeducational vertical, in which the theological academies functioned as higher special theoretical schools of theological education under the spiritual-educational districts. There were four such districts in the Empire – in Kiev, Kazan, Moscow and St. Petersburg. The institutional activity of the Kyiv Theological Academy was influenced by the charters of orthodox theological academies, which were adopted in: 1814, 1869, 1884 and 1910. These charters have established in particular the composition of the staff of the Academy, the number of students, the rules for filling of the vacant positions, awarding of academic degrees and ranks etc. The authorities of the collegial bodies of the Kyiv Theological Academy – the Conference of the Academy, the internal and the external (district) Board of the Academy, which administered directly the Kyiv Theological Academy have also been defined. The higher management system of the Kyiv Theological Academy (rector, inspector) and the mutual relations between them and other objects of the management in the system of higher education of the Russian empire have been described.
explanation: The passage shows an encyclopedic, high-level historical summary with some odd phrasing ('spiritualeducational vertical') that can be characteristic of AI generation or translation; yet the level of detail could also come from a scholarly source.
